content: Interventions addressing social determinants require multi-sectoral approaches beyond traditional healthcare delivery. Evidence-based strategies include: (1) Economic interventions such as earned income tax credits, minimum wage policies, and housing subsidies showing measurable health improvements; (2) Educational interventions including early childhood education programs (e.g., Head Start) demonstrating long-term health benefits; (3) Healthcare access expansions through Medicaid, community health centers, and transportation assistance; (4) Environmental improvements including healthy food access initiatives, safe housing codes, and green space development; (5) Social cohesion building through community organizing and social prescribing programs. Screening for social needs in clinical settings (e.g., PRAPARE tool) should be paired with closed-loop referral systems to community resources. Population-level policy interventions show greater impact than individual-level interventions alone.
